title: Lesson 6 — The Windbag

words.definition: using or containing more words than are needed; wordy
words.example: The verbose report buried its point on page nine.

words.definition: tending to talk a great deal; very talkative
words.example: Our loquacious guide talked for three hours without pausing.

words.definition: sparing in words; brief and often abrupt
expressed clearly in few words; concise
words.example: Her succinct summary fit on a single line.

words.definition: fluent and confident but insincere or shallow
words.example: A glib salesman with an easy answer for everything.

words.definition: pompous or extravagant in language, style, or manner
words.example: His grandiloquent toast used ten words where one would do.
A pithy proverb captured the whole lesson in six words.

words.definition: abrupt or curt in manner or speech, to the point of rudeness
words.example: The brusque clerk waved us away without looking up.

words.definition: a lengthy and aggressive speech; a scolding lecture
words.example: He launched into a harangue about "kids these days."

words.definition: a long, angry speech of criticism or accusation
words.example: Her tirade against the referee got her ejected.

words.definition: to depart from the main subject in speech or writing
words.example: The professor digressed into a long story about his cat.

words.definition: the art of persuasive speaking or writing; language that is impressive but often empty
words.example: Campaign rhetoric rarely survives contact with a real budget.

story: Councilman Pratt was famously [[verbose|verbose]], so [[loquacious|loquacious]] that residents brought snacks to his meetings. Asked a plain question, he never gave a [[terse|terse]] or [[succinct|succinct]] reply; instead he offered [[glib|glib]] promises wrapped in [[grandiloquent|grandiloquent]] phrases. His rival answered in [[pithy|pithy]], almost [[brusque|brusque]] sentences that voters actually remembered. When one resident's [[harangue|harangue]] about potholes swelled into a furious [[tirade|tirade]], Pratt seized the opening to [[digress|digress]] for twenty minutes, mistaking his own [[rhetoric|rhetoric]] for leadership.
